scenario: A team's Web Modeler has 50+ Connectors in the palette; most modelers only use 5. They wonder if the palette can be customised per project / per user to declutter.
question: Connector palette customisation?

options.text: 8 does NOT offer a UI to globally hide them per project. Available customisation knobs: - Project-scoped templates (:51-58): "Publish to project" activates a template in that project's palette. - Organization-scoped templates: "Publish to organization" gives an org-wide curated catalog visible in all projects. - Custom wrapper templates: build templates wrapping standard Connectors with company defaults / auth -- modelers see "Company REST Connector" instead of raw REST. - BPMN linting in CI: enforce usage policies without hiding the palette. For org-wide governance, combine project-scoped + org-scoped Element Templates as the curated catalog and enforce via lint.

Web Modeler provides absolutely no mechanism for palette customisation; every modeler in every project always sees the full list of all available standard Connectors and Elements with no ability to filter, prioritise, or manage the palette. This misrepresents the documented behavior. Web Modeler supports palette customisation through Element Templates published at project or organisation scope. Custom templates can wrap or supplement standard Connectors, and governance policies can be enforced using BPMN linting in CI pipelines. The palette is not completely fixed.

Web Modeler's palette customisation is strictly per-user -- each modeler can configure their personal palette preferences independently, but there is no way to enforce a consistent curated palette across an entire project team or organisation. Web Modeler Element Templates can be published at project scope (visible to all modelers in that project) or at organisation scope (visible across all projects in the organisation). This provides team-level and org-level curation, not just per-user personal settings.

Customising the Web Modeler Connector palette requires forking the Camunda open-source repository, modifying the palette source code, and building a custom Web Modeler distribution; there is no configuration-based approach available. This vastly overstates the complexity required. Web Modeler provides built-in Element Template management functionality at project and organisation scope that achieves palette customisation through configuration, not source code modification. Custom Element Templates are JSON files deployed through the Web Modeler UI, requiring no source code changes to Camunda.

a.text: Correct. Web Modeler palette governance: Standard out-of-the-box Connectors: Always in palette by default. May not be removable globally; may be hidden / deprioritised. Element Template customisation: Deploy custom templates per project. Templates appear in palette / property panel. Curate company-specific patterns (e.g., "Approved Internal REST API Connector"). Restrict pattern usage indirectly. Project-level features (verify): Some Web Modeler versions: project admin controls which Connectors are visible. Reduces choice overload for modelers. Governance patterns: Pattern 1: Approved-only Connectors: Admin curates which standard Connectors are enabled. Modelers see only approved set. Reduces risk of accidental use of restricted services. Pattern 2: Custom wrappers: Build custom Connectors / Templates wrapping standard ones with company defaults / security policies. Modelers see "Company REST Connector" with mandatory auth headers, not raw REST Connector. Pattern 3: Documentation + linting: All Connectors visible; CI / BPMN linting validates usage policies. Modelers educated via internal docs. Pattern 4: Project templates: Process model templates with pre-configured Connectors. Modelers start from template; don't re-pick from full palette. Considerations: Over-restriction: blocks legitimate use cases; forces workarounds. Under-restriction: anarchic palette; inconsistent patterns. Balance: curated set + escape hatch for exceptional needs. Operational: Track which Connectors are used; deprecate truly unused. Survey modelers periodically; align palette with real needs. Verify exact palette customisation features per Camunda 8 / Web Modeler version.
